mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS level was 357841.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4% after a rising 3-month trend, tighter monetary policy raises discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS level was 4.16 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment rises while unemployment falls over the same 3-month window, expanding labor income supports consumption and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS was 134468.0 and UNRATE was 4.9 as of one month before the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with improving labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state does not outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return in at least 55% of walk-forward triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the policy rate is already high, inflation pressure forces restrictive policy and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL was 197.8 and FEDFUNDS was 4.16
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS level is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in industrial production signals deteriorating goods-sector output and weaker operating leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO level was 108.96 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-downside trigger periods are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside-trigger mean returns exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below their 6-month average after a prior expansion, weakening residential investment and related credit demand reduce cyclical earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST level was 2123.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST downside-cross trigger does not predict lower-than-unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment-rate jump signals abrupt labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, causing investors to cut equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 1-month change crosses above 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE level was 4.9 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE jumps are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the median triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth and industrial production growth are both positive, broad demand and production momentum reinforce earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 was 11202.3 as of six months before the decision date and INDPRO was 108.96 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and INDPRO state fails to deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations or if its mean return is below the unconditional mean.
